---
title: "linear_interpolate"
generated: true
description: "Fill masked-out frames via linear interpolation."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.signal.interpolate`](../nitrix-signal-interpolate.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:linear_interpolate -->
```python
linear_interpolate(data: Num[Array, '... obs'], mask: Bool[Array, '... obs']) -> Num[Array, '... obs']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:linear_interpolate -->
Fill masked-out frames via linear interpolation.

For each observation channel, missing frames (`mask == False`)
are replaced by a piecewise-linear interpolation between the
nearest observed frames on either side.  Leading / trailing
missing frames are filled with the nearest observed value
(edge replication).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:linear_interpolate -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `data` | `Num` | `...` × `obs` | — | Time series, observation axis is the last axis. Leading axes are vmapped over independently. |
| `mask` | `Bool` | `...` × `obs` | — | Boolean mask with the same shape; `True` means observed, `False` means missing. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `...` × `obs`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:linear_interpolate -->
::: {.callout-note}
Differentiable through `data`; not through `mask` (a
boolean dispatcher).  Implementation uses two parallel
associative scans (`O(log n)` depth on GPU) rather than the
sequential `lax.scan` of the legacy code.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:linear_interpolate -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `obs` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
